proportionality.description: GDPR applies proportionality through risk-based obligations and SME derogations

tiers.name: full
tiers.applies_to: All controllers and processors handling personal data

tiers.name: reduced_record_keeping
tiers.applies_to: Enterprises with fewer than 250 employees (Art. 30(5) derogation)
tiers.note: Exemption from records of processing UNLESS processing is not occasional, involves special categories, or is likely to result in risk to rights and freedoms

pitfalls.description: DPO appointment is mandatory only in three cases: (a) public authority, (b) core activities require large-scale systematic monitoring, (c) core activities involve large-scale processing of special categories. Not all organizations need a DPO.

Legitimate interest requires a three-part test: (1) identify the legitimate interest, (2) necessity test, (3) balancing test against data subject rights. A bare assertion of legitimate interest is insufficient.

72-hour breach notification to supervisory authority runs from 'becoming aware' of the breach, not from the breach itself. Awareness test is nuanced — controller must have reasonable degree of certainty.

cross_regulation.note: GDPR Art. 28 processor agreements stack with DORA Art. 30 ICT contract provisions for financial entities.
NIS2 Art. 35 incident notification to CSIRT is separate from GDPR Art. 33 breach notification to DPA.

national_implementation.pattern: GDPR is directly applicable. Search Law MCPs for national implementing acts (e.g., BDSG in Germany, AVG in Netherlands, DPA 2018 in UK pre-Brexit).

citation_format: Cite as: GDPR Art. X(Y). For recitals: GDPR Recital N.
